intended_use: TAIMedImg DeepMets is a software device intended to assist trained medical professionals by providing initial object contours on axial T1-weighted contrast-enhanced (T1WI+C) brain magnetic resonance (
intended_use_full: TAIMedImg DeepMets is a software device intended to assist trained medical professionals by providing initial object contours on axial T1-weighted contrast-enhanced (T1WI+C) brain magnetic resonance (MR) images to accelerate workflow for radiation therapy treatment planning. TAIMedImg DeepMets is intended only for patients with known (imaging diagnosed) brain metastases (BM) when cancer cells spread from primary site to the brain. It is not intended to be used with images of other brain tumors or other body parts. The software is intended for use with BM lesions with a diameter of ≥10 mm.
target_condition: Brain metastases (cancer cells spread from primary site to brain)
ground_truth: Manually established consensus annotations by three clinically experienced radiologists/neuroradiologists based on NRG/RTOG clinical guidelines
other_metrics: False-Positive Rate (FPR): 0.354 FPs/case (95% CI: 0.215-0.481); Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC): 0.70 (95% CI: 0.67-0.72); Hausdorff Distance (HD): 6.66 mm (95% CI: 5.86-7.41); Centroid Distance (CD): 1.75 mm (95% CI: 1.33-2.11)
